zh.Sockburn, Durham, England: 英國英格蘭達勒姆索可本
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Conyers於1576年出生在英國英格蘭達勒姆索可本時，她的父親Sir John Conyers32歲，母親Agnes Bowes27歲。她於1593年1月8日與James Conyers Lawson在英國英格蘭達勒姆索可本結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Conyers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire, of Norman origin): habitational name from either of two places in northern France, Coignières in Seine-et-Oise or Cogners in Sarthe. Alternatively, a variant of Conyer , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Conyer
meanings: English: occupational name for a minter, a coiner of money, from an agent derivative of Old French coignier ‘to stamp money, to mint’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Conyers was born in 1576, in Sockburn, Durham, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John Conyers, was 32 and her mother, Agnes Bowes, was 27. She married James Conyers Lawson on 8 January 1593, in Sockburn, Durham, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Conyers est née en 1576, à Sockburn, Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Conyers, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Agnes Bowes, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé James Conyers Lawson le 8 janvier 1593, à Sockburn, Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Conyers 1576 in Sockburn, Durham, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Conyers 32 und ihre Mutter Agnes Bowes 27. Sie hat am 8. Januar 1593 in Sockburn, Durham, England, United Kingdom James Conyers Lawson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Conyers è nata il nel 1576 nel Sockburn, Durham, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir John Conyers, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Agnes Bowes, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato James Conyers Lawson il 8 gennaio 1593, nel Sockburn, Durham, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Conyers(이)가 1576년 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람 속번에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Conyers(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Bowes(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1593년 1월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람 속번에서 James Conyers Lawson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다.
